ground_truth.title: AI for Quality and Efficiency in Radiology
ground_truth.author: Reza Forghani, MD, PhD
ground_truth.main_content: Artificial intelligence (AI) is increasingly being leveraged in radiology to enhance image quality and streamline operations. In busy CT and MRI departments, AI innovations help resolve bottlenecks and improve consistency – ultimately delivering a return on investment (ROI) through higher throughput, fewer repeat scans, and better diagnostic outcomes. Modern "smart" imaging systems integrate AI from the technologist's console to image reconstruction and analysis. The new generation of AI-assisted CT scanners can deliver more consistent results, higher image quality, lower radiation dose, and faster throughput, creating extra time for patient-facing tasks. Simplified Technologist Interface: AI-powered scanner consoles provide an intuitive, guided user experience for technologists. By automating complex settings and workflows, they make advanced scans easier to perform. Benefits include shorter training time and consistent quality and efficiency. Automated Patient Positioning & Scan Planning: AI-driven auto-positioning uses cameras and deep learning to center patients with a single touch. This innovation enhances patient setup with faster setup (one-touch auto-centering significantly cuts positioning time), accurate and repeatable positioning (94% within 2 cm of isocenter), and fast and accurate scan planning through AI-based Anatomical Landmark Detection (ALD). Deep Learning Reconstruction: AI-based image reconstruction improves image clarity and can accelerate scanning. In CT, deep learning algorithms can reduce noise and generate higher-quality images at lower radiation doses. In MRI, they can reduce noise and improve quality while enabling significantly shorter scan times. Automated Post-Processing: Enhanced technologies, including AI, automate many post-scan processing tasks including image reformats. Benefits include time savings, standardized outputs and quicker results, and fast actionable results through zero-click workflow. Conclusion: Across CT and MRI imaging, AI technologies enhance operational efficiency, quality, and safety while addressing operational pain points including staffing shortages. Simplified interfaces, auto-positioning, deep learning reconstructions, and automated post-processing contribute to faster workflows and more reliable results. These improvements allow radiology departments to handle higher volumes with existing resources, yielding ROI through increased throughput, fewer repeat scans, and optimized staff utilization.
